The biggest mistake retail investors make is confusing a listing event with a value creation event.

I have spent years advising at the intersection of debt and equity, and I can tell you that the smartest money in the room isn't looking at Jio's user growth right now. They are looking at the interest coverage ratio. When a conglomerate moves toward an IPO after a 13% profit dip, they aren't trying to share the wealth; they are trying to offload the leverage.

In my experience, an IPO of this magnitude is often less about rewarding the public and more about repairing a consolidated balance sheet. By carving out the debt into a separate entity, the parent company breathes again, but the new shareholders inherit the pressure of servicing that massive capital expenditure.

What the market calls a $133 Billion benchmark is actually an exit ramp for global funds. In a high-interest environment, liquidity is zero-sum, and when capital floods into a perceived safe haven like Jio, it starves the mid-market founders who actually drive innovation. We saw this playbook in the nineties: build on credit, exit on hype.

The takeaway for you is simple: never buy the marketing budget when you should be buying the balance sheet. If the timing of a deal feels forced by external volatility, the valuation is likely priced for the sellers' needs, not your gains.

Follow @thevakul for more.

📊 NLC India Granted Composite Licence for Chhattisgarh Blocks

NLC India Ltd (NLCIL) has been granted composite licences by the Mineral Resources Department, Govt. of Chhattisgarh, for the Semhardih Phosphorite & Limestone Block and Raipura Phosphorite & Limestone Block. This follows NLCIL emerging as the preferred bidder in the Tranche–V auction conducted by the Ministry of Mines and the subsequent submission of Performance Securities to the Government of Chhattisgarh. The licences, issued on April 24, 2026, permit NLCIL to conduct detailed geological exploration in the specified blocks.

📊 Kiran Vyapar: Sukhday Greenview Merged; Ceases to be Subsidiary

Kiran Vyapar Ltd reports that its step-down subsidiary, Sukhday Greenview Private Limited, has been merged with Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited, effective April 25, 2026, following NCLT approval. Sukhday Greenview ceases to be a step-down subsidiary. IOTA Mtech Power LLP, another step-down subsidiary, will receive equity shares of Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited in exchange for its shareholding in Sukhday Greenview. The scheme of amalgamation was sanctioned by the NCLT on March 16, 2026, and filed with the Registrar of Companies on April 25, 2026.

📊 Kiran Vyapar: Placid Ltd Merger Effective; Gets Maharaja Shree Umaid Shares

Kiran Vyapar Ltd reports that its associate company, Placid Limited, has been merged with Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited following NCLT approval. The scheme became effective on April 25, 2026, after filing with the Registrar of Companies. As a result, Placid Limited is dissolved and ceases to be an associate of Kiran Vyapar. Kiran Vyapar will receive equity shares of Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills at a rate of 515 shares for every 1 share held in Placid Limited.

📊 Kiran Vyapar: Placid Ltd Merges with Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills

Kiran Vyapar Ltd. reports that Placid Limited, in which it held a 29.70% stake, has been amalgamated with Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited (MSUM) following NCLT approval. Effective April 25, 2026, Kiran Vyapar will receive 8,21,55,375 equity shares of ₹10 each in MSUM as consideration, resulting in a total holding of 8,97,29,943 shares, representing approximately 27.73% of MSUM's post-amalgamation equity. Consequently, MSUM will be classified as an associate of Kiran Vyapar.

📊 Kiran Vyapar: Promoter Group Merger Boosts Shareholding to 54.72%

Kiran Vyapar Ltd. announced that Placid Limited, a Promoter Group Company, has been amalgamated with Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited (MSUM) effective April 25, 2026, as per the Scheme of Amalgamation approved by the National Company Law Tribunal, Kolkata. Consequently, Placid Limited stands dissolved, and its 33.86% equity shareholding in Kiran Vyapar Ltd. has been transferred to Maharaja Shree Umaid Mills Limited. Post-acquisition, MSUM's shareholding in Kiran Vyapar Ltd. increases to 54.72%, making it the Holding Company.
